mnemonics: P.I.N. Players, Incentives, No communication: The three core components of a strategic game.
Nash Equilibrium: No player wants to DEVIATE (D.E.V.I.A.T.E. - Doesn't Enjoy Value In Altering Their Existing strategy).
keyPoints: A strategic game is a model of interaction with players, strategies, and payoffs.
The solution concept for these games is the Nash Equilibrium, where no player can benefit by unilaterally changing their strategy.
Players are rational and act to maximize their own payoff, given their beliefs about other players' actions.
commonMistakes: Assuming players can communicate or make binding agreements (this is non-cooperative game theory).
Confusing a dominant strategy (best no matter what others do) with a Nash Equilibrium (best given what others *are* doing).
